Email 1 — I've Asked This Question 10,000 Times. The Answer Never Changes.
advantageANYWHERE
EMAIL 1 OF 5

Hi [First Name],

After 20+ years selling into Long-Term Care and Senior Living — walking the halls, sitting in the family meetings, watching operators do everything right — I've asked a version of the same question ten thousand times.

"What's your biggest challenge right now?"

The answer never changes.

"Census. Staffing. And census."

Census first. Census last. And somewhere in the middle — staffing — because nobody can drive census without the people to execute on it.

Here's what I've learned: most operators losing census aren't losing it on the floor. They're losing it in the funnel. A family calls. Someone takes a message. The follow-up doesn't happen in the first 48 hours. The lead goes cold. The bed stays empty.

Leads don't die because your community isn't excellent.
They die because no system owns them after the first contact.

That's the problem Advantage Anywhere was built to solve — and it's the reason I reached out to you today.

I'm not here to sell you software. I'm here because I've watched this exact problem cost operators census they could have kept — and I finally have something that fixes it.

More in the next email. But if you're already seeing this in your funnel, reply and let me know. I read every one.

~Steven

Email 2 — Your CRM Knows the Stage. It Has No Idea What To Do Next.
advantageANYWHERE
EMAIL 2 OF 5

Hi [First Name],

Your CRM knows what stage every lead is in.

Aline. Continuum. Eldermark. WellSky. They all do this — and they all do it well. You can open any one of them right now and see exactly where every prospect sits in your pipeline.

But here's the question none of them answer:

What is your leasing coordinator supposed to do about it — right now, today, with that specific lead?

The CRM shows the stage. It doesn't run the play.

So what happens? The coordinator remembers the follow-up — or doesn't. Sends the video — or forgets. Makes the call — or gets pulled into something else. The outcome depends on who showed up to work that day and how much they remember to do without being told.

That's not a staffing problem. That's a systems problem.

And a systems problem has a systems solution.

Advantage Anywhere is built differently. It doesn't track what stage a lead is in — it tells your team exactly what to do at every stage. The email, the script, the video, the follow-up timing — all of it scripted, sequenced, and tracked. The stage doesn't advance until the work is done.

Your average leasing coordinator performing like your best one. Every lead. Every time.

That's not a CRM. That's a different category.

Tomorrow I'll tell you about the moment most communities lose the deal — and it's not where you think.

~Steven

Email 3 — The Tour Went Great. The Family Loved It. You Never Heard From Them Again.
advantageANYWHERE
EMAIL 3 OF 5

Hi [First Name],

The tour went great.

The family loved the dining room. They asked all the right questions. Your coordinator walked them through the whole community, answered everything with confidence, and they left saying they'd be in touch.

Two weeks later, they signed with someone else.

The tour didn't lose the deal. The follow-up did.

In every building I've spent time in over the past 20+ years, follow-up is where census goes to die. Not dramatically. Quietly. One family at a time, one unanswered call at a time, one "I'll send that email tomorrow" that never goes out.

The hard truth is that most communities don't lose to better competitors. They lose to faster ones. The family who toured three places signed with the one that stayed in front of them — consistently, personally, without dropping the ball.

Advantage Anywhere automates the follow-up cadence — the calls, the texts, the emails, the check-ins — so nothing falls through after a tour. Your team still builds the relationship. The system makes sure they never go silent.

One operator told me after 90 days: "We didn't get more inquiries. We just stopped losing the ones we already had."

That's census. Without a single new marketing dollar.

~Steven

Email 4 — There's a Person in Your Building Carrying Your Census. What Happens When They Leave?
advantageANYWHERE
EMAIL 4 OF 5

Hi [First Name],

Every building has one.

The leasing coordinator who closes everything. Remembers every family's name. Follows up without being asked. Makes it feel effortless — and quietly carries more move-ins than anyone else on the team.

Now ask yourself: what happens to your census when they take a vacation? Call in sick? Give notice?

Results built on one person aren't results. They're risk.

Here's what I've learned working with LTC operators across the country: the best performers aren't doing anything magical. They're doing the right things, in the right order, consistently, without forgetting. That's not talent. That's a system — and systems can be replicated.

Advantage Anywhere takes what your best closer does naturally and builds it into the platform for everyone else.

Same follow-up cadence. Same scripted touchpoints. Same sequenced actions — for every lead, regardless of who's working that day.

When your whole team executes like your best team member, census stops depending on who showed up. It depends on the system. And systems don't have bad weeks.

One more email coming. It's a short ask — and it might be the most valuable 20 minutes you spend this quarter.

~Steven

Email 5 — Twenty Minutes. No Pitch Deck. Just Two People Who Care About This Space.
advantageANYWHERE
EMAIL 5 OF 5

Hi [First Name],

I'll keep this one short.

Over the past few weeks I've shared what I've seen after 20+ years in this space: census sitting undiscovered in funnels that could be running better, follow-up gaps that cost operators move-ins they never knew they were losing, and a category of tool that actually closes those gaps instead of just tracking them.

I'm not asking you to buy anything.

I'm asking for twenty minutes — no pitch deck, no canned presentation. Just a real conversation between two people who care about what happens to census in this industry.

If what I've described sounds familiar — if you've watched leads go quiet after a good tour, or wondered why move-ins aren't tracking with inquiry volume — that's the conversation I want to have.
Twenty minutes. You pick the time. If it's not a fit, I'll tell you straight — and you'll walk away with at least one thing you can use in your funnel today, regardless.

Click below to find a time that works. I look forward to the conversation.

~Steven
